items.hook: Geometry of quantum systems is mainly phrased in algebraic terms of modules and algebras.
items.body: Connections on modules are generalization of a linear connection on a smooth vector bundle written as a Koszul connection on the -module of sections of . Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Watch the total solar eclipse with ESA in León
items.body: Discover the science behind one of nature's most extraordinary phenomena and experience a total solar eclipse with the European Space Agency (ESA), the City of León and the University of León.

items.hook: General Muhammad Naguib leads the Free Officers Movement (formed by Gamal Abdel Nasser, the real power behind the coup) in overthrowing King Farouk of Egypt.
items.body: Major General Mohamed Bey Naguib Youssef Qutb El-Qashlan, known simply as Mohamed Naguib, was a Sudanese-born Egyptian military officer and revolutionary who, along with Gamal Abdel Nasser, was one of the two principal leaders of the Free Officers movement of 1952 that toppled the monarchy of Egypt and the Sudan, leading to the establishment of the Republic of Egypt. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Seinfeld actor apologises to reality star for 'inappropriate' sketch when she was 17
items.body: Jason Alexander said he "truly regrets" the Funny Or Die sketch he appeared in with Courtney Stodden.

items.hook: Detroit Riots: In Detroit, one of the worst riots in United States history begins on 12th Street in the predominantly African American inner city. It ultimately kills 43 people, injures 342 and burns about 1,400 buildings.
items.body: The 1967 Detroit riot, also known as the 12th Street Riot and the Detroit Uprising, was the bloodiest of the urban riots in the United States during the "long, hot summer of 1967". The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Megawati Sukarnoputri was sworn in as the first female president of Indonesia following her predecessor's impeachment.
items.body: Diah Permata Megawati Setiawati Sukarnoputri is an Indonesian politician who served as the fifth president of Indonesia from 2001 to 2004 and the eighth vice president under President Abdurrahman Wahid from 1999 to 2001. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Dracula 3D is a 2012 vampire horror film directed by Dario Argento from a screenplay he wrote with Enrique Cerezo, Stefano Piani, and Stefano Piani.
items.body: It stars Thomas Kretschmann as Count Dracula, alongside Marta Gastini, Asia Argento, Unax Ugalde, Miriam Giovanelli, and Rutger Hauer. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: The Odyssey film fans inspired to go back to the source
items.body: Book sales and audiobook figures for Homer's original poem rise sharply after the film's release.

items.hook: The Ford Motor Company sells its first car.
items.body: The Ford Motor Company is an American multinational automobile manufacturer headquartered in Dearborn, Michigan, United States. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Bulgarian poet and communist activist Nikola Vaptsarov is executed by firing squad.
items.body: Bulgaria, officially the Republic of Bulgaria, is a country in Southeastern Europe. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: "Fallin' In Love" is a song by American music trio Hamilton, Joe Frank & Reynolds.

items.hook: Lancaster University is a collegiate public research university in Lancaster, Lancashire, England.
items.body: The university was established in 1964 by royal charter, as one of several new universities created in the 1960s. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: The election of Pope John V following the death of Pope Benedict II two months prior.
items.body: Pope John V was the bishop of Rome from 23 July 685 to his death on 2 August 686. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: On Thin Ice by Charlie Walker review – a Siberian adventure goes off the rails
items.body:
Moments of beauty and horror punctuate this account of an ill-timed sub-zero camping trip
